# This Notebook can be used as the template for creating a new Notebook, or for taking the sections of code required to update an existing
# Notebook with an appropriate Feature Flag implemented.

# Feature Flags must be instantiated at the top of your Notebooks - this allows you to implement the Feature Flag cell(s) below before
# the flag itself has been configured in the Unify database. Assiging it to False is mandatory to ensure old code always runs by default.

test_feature_flag = False
blank_feature_flag = False
new_notebook_feature_flag = False
Run the get_feature_flags Notebook to fetch all Feature Flags from the Unify database

# Assign the configured state of your Feature Flag based on the name of the flag in the Unify database.
# The try statement allows for code to continue to run despite unconfigured / non-existent flags.
# This cell can be copied into an existing Notebook, in any cell in that Notebook, where code is to be updated.
# Simply copy the below and paste in the top of the cell being updated and indent the existing code to fit within the else statement.
try:
    test_feature_flag = df_feature_flags.where("FlagName = 'TestFeature'").collect()[0]["FlagState"]
except Exception as e:
    print(e)

# Use your Feature Flag in your code as below
if test_feature_flag:
    print("Feature Flag turned on, new code allowed to run.")
    # place new code here

else:
    print("Feature Flag turned off, new code not allowed to run.")
    # place old code here
# Using the cell above means everything from this cell onwards is protected from running. The below print() statement never completes.
print("This cell will never run because the Feature Flag above is not configured or in the True state.")
